qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unrestricted SSRF in workflow import-from-url endpoint allows internal network scanning and data exfiltration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory concerns improper host file access controls that allow authenticated workflow authors to read sensitive files through filesystem-capable nodes such as Read/Write Files from Disk and Git. This finding concerns a different endpoint and root cause: GET /rest/workflows/from-url passing an attacker-controlled URL to axios.get(query.url), enabling SSRF.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The target advisory is an arbitrary host file read caused by improper file access controls for authenticated users with workflow creation or editing privileges, with workarounds focused on filesystem-interacting nodes. The only final qualifying finding is an open-world SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gfvg-qv54-r4pc summary: n8n's improper file access controls allow arbitrary file read by authenticated users.

Advisory details describe authenticated users with workflow create or modify permission reading sensitive files from the n8n host system, with mitigations to restrict Read/Write Files from Disk and Git nodes.
Finding ssrf-workflow-from-url reports GET /rest/workflows/from-url and packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts:321 axios.get(query.url) as the vulnerable operation.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists ssrf-workflow-from-url as the only final qualifying_vuln finding for this session.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so advisory-specific PoC execution was not applicable.
